title: ZXPong Game Modes
description: Compare ZXPong Solo play and World PVP, with release notes for hidden Local PVP code that is not exposed in the current public menu.
summary: ZXPong release-facing play is centered on Solo play and World PVP.
heroImageAlt: Placeholder artwork for pending ZXPong game modes image
sourceNotes: Current source has `enableWorldPVP = true` and `enableLocalPVP = false`.
Local PVP is retained in code but hidden from the release-facing menu. Do not advertise it as a public mode until the flag changes.

sections.heading: Solo play
paragraphs: Solo play is the practical starting point for learning paddle control, rally timing, and scoring rhythm.
The current source includes AI difficulty selection with levels 1 through 10, which changes how strongly the AI responds.

sections.heading: World PVP
paragraphs: World PVP is the visible multiplayer route in the current release-facing build.
Use World PVP after you are comfortable with paddle timing and can keep rallies stable under pressure.

sections.heading: Local PVP status
paragraphs: Local PVP support is retained in code, but the current release-facing feature flag hides it from the menu.
This documentation does not present Local PVP as a public mode until the release configuration changes.

images.alt: Placeholder artwork for ZXPong game modes
images.caption: Game modes artwork pending

faq.question: Is Local PVP available in the current release-facing menu?
faq.answer: No. Current source keeps Local PVP hidden through the release-facing feature flag, while World PVP is enabled.
